title: Direct $_GET access instead of $params in action file
description: An action file accesses $_GET directly. In CMSMS action files, frontend parameters arrive via $params. $_POST and $_REQUEST access is acceptable for direct input cleaning per CMSMS 2.2.8+ guidance.
explanation: CMSMS auto-cleans $_GET and delivers action parameters via $params. Direct $_GET access in action files bypasses module parameter scoping and may read parameters intended for other modules. $_POST/$_REQUEST access is acceptable since CMSMS 2.2.8 deprecated SetParameterType() in favor of direct input cleaning.
fix: Use $params['key'] for action parameters. For direct input cleaning, use filter_input(INPUT_GET, 'key', FILTER_SANITIZE_...) instead of $_GET['key'].
